2024 SCC 1 [2024] 1 SCR 42 [2024] 1 S.C.R. 42
Supreme Court of Canada 2024
2024-01-15
Wagner C.J. Karakatsanis J. Côté J.

Issues

1

The appellant challenges the trial judge's ruling on the admissibility of evidence seized during a warrantless search under s. 8 of the Charter.

Holding

2

The Court holds that the search was unreasonable. The evidence is excluded under s. 24(2) of the Charter.

Reasons

3

This appeal concerns the scope of the reasonable expectation of privacy in shared digital storage devices accessed during an unrelated investigation.

4

The trial judge found that the police seized the appellant's laptop without a warrant and reviewed its contents over the course of three days.

5

The Crown submits that the appellant had abandoned any reasonable expectation of privacy by leaving the device unattended at a public library terminal.

6

We disagree. Abandonment requires intent, not mere physical separation. The appellant retained a subjective expectation that the contents would remain private, and that expectation was objectively reasonable in the circumstances.

Conclusion

7

The appeal is allowed. The conviction is set aside and a new trial is ordered.
